name: La Sierra de Mollerussa - Vilanova de Bellpuig  — circular by the Pla de Urgell
name_original: La Sierra de Mollerussa - Vilanova de Bellpuig  — circular por el Pla de Urgell
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mollerussa offers routes across flat, well-paved surfaces, ideal for exploring the region's agricultural heartland. The landscape is characterized by extensive plains, historic irrigation canals, and significant natural ponds. These features provide a tranquil backdrop for cycling, with minimal elevation changes on many routes. Best touring cycling routes around Mollerussa The most popular touring cycling route is La Sierra de Mollerussa - Vilanova de Bellpuig — circular by the Pla de Urgell, a 16.8 miles (27.1 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete, offering a circular route through the Pla de Urgell region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bell-lloc d'Urgell – Fita Alta Summit loop from Mollerussa, a difficult 27.5 miles (44.3 km) path. This route features varied terrain and includes a summit viewpoint. Local touring cyclists also love the Church of Sant Martí de Maldà – Bellpuig Castle loop from Mollerussa, a 36.3 miles (58.4 km) trail leading through historical sites and cultural landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Mollerussa is defined by agricultural plains, historic canals, and natural ponds. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisure rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Mollerussa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mollerussa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Mollerussa?
A: The touring cycling routes around Mollerussa are primarily characterized by flat, well-paved surfaces, making them ideal for exploring the region's agricultural heartland. You'll encounter extensive plains, historic irrigation canals, and natural ponds, with minimal elevation changes on many routes. Some sections, especially along the Canal d'Urgell, are suitable for gravel or mountain bikes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Mollerussa offers several easy routes. For instance, the region around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; provides an easy 40.6 km road cycling tour, perfect for nature lovers and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3668455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Third Canal of Urgell Path&lt;/a&gt; also offers flat, scenic, and traffic-free sections ideal for leisure cyclists.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while cycling in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa is rich in natural features. You can cycle around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, the largest pond in Catalonia, known for its biodiversity and birdwatching opportunities. The historic Canal d'Urgell also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a popular spot for a break. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant forest setting with picnic areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available from Mollerussa?
A: Yes, many routes around Mollerussa are circular. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962991750/la-sierra-de-mollerussa-vilanova-de-bellpuig-circular-by-the-pla-de-urgell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra de Mollerussa - Vilanova de Bellpuig — circular by the Pla de Urgell&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 27.1 km. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bell-lloc d'Urgell – Fita Alta Summit loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, a more difficult 44.3 km path with varied terrain.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit on a touring cycling route?
A: Mollerussa's cycling routes often pass through villages with historical significance. You can explore routes that lead to sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Martí de Maldà – Bellpuig Castle loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through historical sites and cultural landmarks. The region also features numerous castles and Romanesque churches, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year due to its mild climate. Spring and autumn offer comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summers can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable. Winters are generally mild, allowing for cycling, though you should check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more experienced touring cyclists, there are challenging routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassella Reservoir – Parish Church of Juneda loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 81.8 km route with significant elevation gain, offering a substantial challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bell-lloc d'Urgell – Fita Alta Summit loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt; is also rated difficult, featuring varied terrain and a summit viewpoint.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mollerussa?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes available around Mollerussa, catering to various fitness levels. This includes 12 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 28 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from leisure riders to those seeking a challenge.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Mollerussa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mollerussa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the flat, well-paved surfaces and the tranquil agricultural landscapes.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or pubs along the cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes and pubs are not listed for every route, Mollerussa and the villages traversed by the cycling routes offer various amenities. You'll find opportunities to stop for refreshments and meals, especially in the larger towns and villages along your journey through the Pla d'Urgell region.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by bike in the Mollerussa area?
A: Yes, the Mollerussa area features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.5-meter waterfall located along the historic Canal d'Urgell. This is a popular spot for cyclists and hikers alike, offering a scenic break during your ride through the agricultural plains.

Q: Is Mollerussa a good base for multi-day cycling tours?
A: Mollerussa serves as an excellent base for multi-day cycling excursions. The region offers a network of routes, including the Mollerussa-Linyola-Bellpuig route, which covers 73.6 km and traverses various villages, providing a mix of quiet roads and tracks suitable for extended exploration of the Pla d'Urgell region.
tips:
In the town of Mollerussa, we find this 19th century building and it is the Cultural Space of the Urgell Canals, where we can learn about the Urgell canal, the land and its history thanks to the museum.
It is advisable to go with a prior visit.

Idyllic place to spend the day with children, as a couple or as a family.
Under the shade of the trees, we have a picnic area, with benches, tables and barbecues.
There is also a small fenced pond with ducks and turtles.
Not far away is the Sierra de Mollerussa Rally circuit.

The Patano de la Bassella gives us a beautiful view, especially if we arrive at dusk.
It is located near Tossal del Infern (a small mountain of 301 meters in Pla d'Urgell)

We are in Vilanova de Bellpuig, next to the parish of Sant Pere, the current church was built at the beginning of the last century, taking advantage of the structure of the old building dating from the fourteenth century, built in Gothic style. The building also houses the altarpiece of the Virgen del Lliri, from the 14th century, one of the most valuable artistic elements in the Pla d'Urgell region.

Popular destination for hiking or biking among locals. It is a beautiful waterfall of 4.5 meters that we find in the third drought of the Urgell channel.

For years it provided electricity to a large company in the town of Mollerussa, which was the engine of its economy.
